target_roosting_group: Small-to-medium North American crevice-roosting colonial bats
example_species_only_not_species_locked: Mexican free-tailed bat (Tadarida brasiliensis) in suitable warm regions
explicitly_not_for: Sites with extreme summer heat and no shade or paired thermal option

references.name: Batweek - How to Install a Bat House

primary.fasteners: Exterior-rated screws, washers, and lag hardware
primary.adhesive: Exterior wood glue or sealant only where squeeze-out cannot enter roost chambers
primary.exterior_finish: Water-based exterior paint or stain on exterior only

alternate.all_exterior_surfaces: 19 mm cedar boards, edge-joined as needed
alternate.tradeoff: Better outdoor durability, slower fabrication, more joinery and seasonal movement management

warm.sun_exposure: morning to mid-day sun; avoid full late-afternoon bake

very_hot.exterior_color: light tan, light gray, or off-white
very_hot.sun_exposure: morning sun plus afternoon shade or paired thermal alternatives
very_hot.venting: vents required; site-specific overheating check required
very_hot.note: Do not deploy the default package in extreme heat without thermal tuning.

roughening.method: horizontal saw kerfs or dense router grooves
required_surfaces: inside face of lower front panel
all exposed landing surfaces below the lower front

weather_and_finish.top_seams: Seal exterior roof and top-edge seams.
weather_and_finish.bottom: Leave fully open; do not caulk shut.
weather_and_finish.interior_seams: Avoid squeeze-out that creates slick surfaces.
weather_and_finish.flashing: Optional thin cap over roof for wet climates.

mounting.alternate: Back-to-back pole pair with predator baffle and no branch access
disallowed_default: metal building wall without thermal backing
site with branch or roof access for predators
site with no clear drop space below exit
mounting.orientation_note: Prioritize morning sun and stable daytime warmth; adjust azimuth to regional climate.

maintenance.inspection_timing: Inspect and repair when bats are absent, typically winter or after local maternity season.
maintenance.do_not_disturb: Do not open, clean, or rework an occupied roost.
maintenance.guano: Plan for guano below the open bottom; do not mount above doors, seating, grills, or food prep.

welfare_gates.roost_gap: REQUIRED: 19-25 mm clear chamber gaps; default 19 mm.
welfare_gates.roughened_landing: REQUIRED: roughened landing and roost surfaces.
welfare_gates.venting: REQUIRED: front and side venting unless a cold-climate variant documents why vents are omitted.
welfare_gates.clear_drop_space: REQUIRED: at least 12 ft clear drop below exit.
welfare_gates.no_mesh: FORBIDDEN: no stapled mesh, screen, or netting on roost surfaces.
welfare_gates.no_interior_finish: FORBIDDEN: no paint, stain, oil, or sealer inside.
welfare_gates.no_treated_interior_lumber: FORBIDDEN: treated or chemically finished interior lumber.
welfare_gates.tree_mount_discouraged: REQUIRED: tree mount is disallowed as the default recommendation.
welfare_gates.maintenance_timing: REQUIRED: inspect or repair only when bats are absent.
